description: Persistence & Debug Menu — Full game state save/load with auto-save to Neon DB, versioned save format with migrations, and comprehensive debug menu for rapid developer iteration.

userStories.title: GameState central store with change detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a central state store that tracks all game data with change detection so that UI updates reactively and serialization captures everything.
acceptanceCriteria: src/state/GameState.ts exposes: get<T>(path: string): T, set<T>(path: string, value: T), onChange<T>(path: string, callback): Unsubscribe, serialize(): SaveData, restore(data: SaveData)

State shape tracks: player (appearance, position, stats.level, stats.health, stats.maxHealth, yarn, oxygen, inventory), world (currentMapId, activeCats array, hiddenTerrain array, resourceNodes array), session (totalPlaytimeMs, isSwimming, isDiving)
set() triggers registered onChange() callbacks for the matching path
serialize() excludes transient session state and debug overrides, returns JSON-safe object
restore() hydrates all state from saved snapshot and triggers all change callbacks
Validation: set() rejects invalid values (negative health, yarn below 0) by clamping or ignoring
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SaveData format, types, and version migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a versioned save format so that future schema changes don't break existing saves.
acceptanceCriteria: src/modules/persistence/types.ts defines SaveData interface: version string, character (appearance, stats, inventory items, position), world (activeCats with type/position, resourceNodeCooldowns), session (totalPlaytimeMs)
src/modules/persistence/migrations.ts implements migrateIfNeeded(data: unknown): SaveData
Migration chain pattern: version '0.1' -> '0.2' -> ... each with a transform function in a Record
If version matches current: returns data as-is. If older: chains migrations sequentially. If unknown future version: throws descriptive error
Stub migration from '0.1' to '0.2' included as template showing the pattern
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persistence module — save, load, auto-save with dirty tracking
userStories.description: As a player, I want my game to auto-save so that I never lose progress.
acceptanceCriteria: src/state/Persistence.ts exposes: save(): Promise<void>, load(): Promise<SaveData | null>, startAutoSave(intervalMs), stopAutoSave(), forceSave(): Promise<void>, isSaving (bool), lastSavedAt (Date | null)
save() calls gameState.serialize() and sends to Neon game_saves table via NeonClient.upsert()
load() fetches from Neon game_saves table, runs migrateIfNeeded(), returns SaveData or null
startAutoSave(30000) sets interval that calls save() only when isDirty flag is true
isDirty flag: set to true by GameState.set() calls, cleared after successful save
Position updates only set isDirty every 5 seconds (quantized, prevents excessive saves from movement)
forceSave() saves immediately regardless of dirty flag
window.beforeunload handler calls forceSave() using navigator.sendBeacon() as fallback
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Load and restore flow on app start
userStories.description: As a returning player, I want to resume where I left off when I open the game.
acceptanceCriteria: On app start after auth: Persistence.load() called automatically
If no save AND no character in DB: redirect to Character Creator
If character exists but no save: start new game at map spawn point with default state (10 yarn, full health, empty inventory)
If save exists: gameState.restore(saveData) called, player entity spawned at saved position with saved stats, inventory, yarn
Active cats from save re-summoned at saved positions via CatCompanionManager (invalid positions cause silent dismiss)
Resource node cooldowns restored (nodes on cooldown remain on cooldown with remaining time)
Loading screen shown while fetching from Neon
If load fails (network error): show retry button and allow starting new game as fallback
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Debug menu shell with tabs and toggle
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a debug menu overlay that I can toggle on and off during gameplay.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ui/DebugMenu.ts renders when Ctrl+D is pressed (only in import.meta.env.DEV builds)
Semi-transparent dark panel overlaying game canvas (does NOT pause game)
Organized in 4 tabs: Player, Cats, World, Session — clicking tab name switches visible content
Ctrl+D toggles menu closed
Menu has high z-index (renders above canvas and HUD)
All debug value changes emit DEBUG_VALUE_CHANGED events on EventBus
Debug values are session-only — never included in GameState.serialize()
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Debug menu — Player tab controls
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to modify player stats in real-time so I can test different progression states.
acceptanceCriteria: Player tab contains: Level selector (number input 0-10), Health setter (slider 0 to maxHealth), Yarn setter (number input 0-99), Teleport fields (X and Z coordinate inputs + Teleport button), Speed multiplier slider (0.5x to 3.0x that modifies runtimeConfig.movement.walkSpeed), God mode checkbox (prevents health loss)
Level change updates GameState player.stats.level immediately
Health change updates GameState player.stats.health immediately
Yarn change updates GameState player.yarn immediately
Teleport button moves player entity Transform to specified coordinates
Speed multiplier modifies runtimeConfig and is read by MovementSystem next frame
God mode flag checked by oxygen/damage systems to skip health reduction
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Debug menu — Cats tab controls
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to test cat abilities without resource constraints so I can iterate on cat behavior rapidly.
acceptanceCriteria: Cats tab contains: Force-summon button for each cat type (Loaf, Zoomies, CuriosityCat, Pounce — ignores yarn cost and cat limit), Yarn override number input, Cat limit override number input (1-10), Dismiss All button, Active cats list showing each cat's type and CatBehavior state
Force-summon spawns cat at player position + 2u offset in facing direction
Yarn override sets GameState player.yarn immediately
Cat limit override modifies runtimeConfig cats.maxActive
Dismiss All calls CatCompanionManager.dismiss() for every active companion
Active cats list updates in real-time as cats are summoned/dismissed/expire
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Debug menu — World tab controls
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to manipulate world state so I can test edge cases and balance.
acceptanceCriteria: World tab contains: Time scale slider (0.25x to 4.0x), Reload Map button, Show Collision Wireframes toggle, Entity Count display, Fill All Nodes button (resets all resource cooldowns to 0), Empty All Nodes button (sets all cooldowns to max)
Time scale multiplies dt at Game loop level before passing to systems (InputManager NOT scaled)
Reload Map calls MapManager.loadMap(TestMap) to reset all terrain
Collision wireframes toggle adds/removes wireframe meshes for all Collider entities via SceneManager
Entity count displays total and breakdown by component type (PlayerControlled, CatBehavior, ResourceNode, etc.)
Fill/Empty nodes iterates all entities with ResourceNode component and modifies cooldownRemaining
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Debug menu — Session tab controls
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to control save/load and view session diagnostics.
acceptanceCriteria: Session tab contains: Force Save button (calls Persistence.forceSave(), shows success/failure text), Force Load button (calls Persistence.load() + gameState.restore()), Reset All State button with confirmation dialog, Last Saved timestamp display, FPS counter, Entity count, Session playtime display, Auto-save toggle (enabled/disabled)
Force Save shows 'Saved!' or 'Save failed: [error]' text after completion
Force Load reloads entire game state from DB
Reset All State: shows 'Are you sure?' confirm dialog, then deletes save from DB via NeonClient.delete() and reloads game with default state (keeps character)
Last Saved timestamp updates reactively from Persistence.lastSavedAt
FPS counter updates every second with rolling average
Auto-save toggle calls Persistence.startAutoSave() or stopAutoSave()
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: HUD save indicator
userStories.description: As a player, I want subtle save feedback so I know my progress is being saved without being interrupted.
acceptanceCriteria: Small save icon (disk/floppy icon as text or simple SVG) appears in bottom-right corner when auto-save completes successfully
Icon shows for 2 seconds then fades out (CSS opacity transition)
On save failure: icon shows with red color and X mark, persists for 5 seconds
Save indicator does not block gameplay or overlap with other HUD elements
Triggered by listening to SAVE_COMPLETED event on EventBus
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
